meta.description: Practice starts at 169 strategically distinct starting-hand classes. Combo-level drilling can later expand that to all 1,326 two-card combinations.

cbet.prompt: You opened the button and got called by the BB. Flop A-7-2 rainbow. What's the best practical default?
cbet.explanation: Dry ace-high board gives the preflop raiser a clear range and nut advantage. Small c-bet at high frequency is the clean default.

cbet.prompt: Cutoff opened, BB called. Flop K-8-3 rainbow. Default?
cbet.explanation: Another dry high-card board where your range leans stronger than the defender's. Small size keeps the whole range printing.

cbet.prompt: You 3-bet BTN vs CO and got called. Flop A-8-4 rainbow. Best default?
cbet.explanation: 3-bet pots on dry ace-high textures heavily favor the aggressor. Small sizing pressures misses and realizes equity cleanly.

cbet.prompt: Blind vs blind, SB opened and BB called. Flop Q-6-2 rainbow. Good default?
cbet.explanation: Even blind vs blind, this dry queen-high board still lets the opener lean into a cheap range c-bet.

cbet.prompt: Button opened, BB defended. Flop T-8-6 with two clubs. Best practical default?
cbet.explanation: This board smashes the BB's defend range. Too connected and too many pair+draw combos for mindless c-betting.

cbet.prompt: UTG opened, BB called. Flop 9-7-5 with a flush draw. What's the better default?
cbet.explanation: Low connected boards pull the defender closer to parity. Checking protects your range and avoids lighting money on fire.

cbet.prompt: Cutoff opened, BB called. Flop K-J-4 with a flush draw. Practical default?
cbet.explanation: Still enough top-card advantage to c-bet often, but use a controlled size instead of blasting into all the draws.

cbet.prompt: You 3-bet BTN vs HJ and get called. Flop Q-T-5 with a flush draw. Default?
cbet.explanation: Broadway connectivity plus flush draws gives the caller real coverage here. Checking more is the disciplined play.

cbet.prompt: BTN opened, BB defended. Flop A-J-9 with a heart draw. What's your best default?
cbet.explanation: This is closer than A-7-2, but you still keep a real ace-high advantage. Small size works better than over-correcting to a check.

cbet.prompt: Blind vs blind on 8-6-4 two-heart. Default?
cbet.explanation: BB smashes this board with pair+draw, straight draws, and combo draws. This is a classic check-more texture.

cbet.prompt: You 3-bet BTN vs CO and get called. Flop K-7-2 with a club draw. Best practical line?
cbet.explanation: You still retain a strong range edge in the 3-bet pot. The draw adds texture, but not enough to force a pure check.

cbet.prompt: Cutoff opened, BB defended. Flop J-9-8 with a flush draw. What's the disciplined default?
cbet.explanation: Very dynamic board, lots of two-pair, pair+draw, and combo-draw coverage for BB. This is not a board to auto-fire.

shoveFold.prompt: 8bb on the button, folded to you. What's the play?
shoveFold.explanation: Classic profitable button shove. Suited ace with blocker value and strong equity when called.

shoveFold.prompt: 7bb in the small blind, folded to you. Best move?
shoveFold.explanation: Blind-vs-blind at 7bb is an aggression tax spot. KTs is comfortably strong enough to rip.

shoveFold.prompt: 9bb in the cutoff, folded to you. What now?
shoveFold.explanation: Mid pairs at 9bb are textbook jams from late position. Too strong to pass, awkward to min-raise/fold.

shoveFold.prompt: 10bb in the hijack, folded to you. Jam or fold?
shoveFold.explanation: Too loose from HJ. Offsuit queen-rag doesn't have the blocker/equity mix needed at 10bb.

shoveFold.prompt: 11bb on the button, folded to you. What's better?
shoveFold.explanation: Still profitable enough on the button against normal blinds. Strong king blocker plus suitedness carries it.

shoveFold.prompt: 12bb in the cutoff, action folds to you. Jam or fold?
shoveFold.explanation: A5o uses ace blocker power well and still has acceptable called equity. Practical jam at this depth.

shoveFold.prompt: 9bb in the SB, folded to you. What's the move?
shoveFold.explanation: Blind-vs-blind widens fast. J7o is ugly, but still jams profitably at 9bb once BTN folds.

shoveFold.prompt: 12bb in the hijack, folded to you. Rip it or pass?
shoveFold.explanation: Too loose that early with 12bb. This hand looks prettier than it plays once called.

shoveFold.prompt: 14bb in the cutoff, action folds to you. Jam or fold?
shoveFold.explanation: This gets close, but small pairs still make money as open jams often enough at 14bb in late position.

shoveFold.prompt: 13bb on the button, folded to you. Ship it?
shoveFold.explanation: Borderline-but-good late-position jam. Suited queen with live-card equity gets over the line.

shoveFold.prompt: 11bb in the SB, folded to you. Jam or let it go?
shoveFold.explanation: Blind-vs-blind keeps this alive. Suiting plus immediate fold equity makes it a practical jam.

shoveFold.prompt: 10bb UTG, folded to you. Jam or fold?
shoveFold.explanation: Ace blocker is nice, but position matters. UTG jamming range is tighter and A4o falls just outside the comfort zone.
